Sorbus aucuparia is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.

The bioactive constituents of Sorbus aucuparia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Sorbus aucuparia include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues.
